title: Dynamic lighting
description: Make fire/lava cast light: screen-space radial glow brightening nearby cells. Second additive render pass on the existing glow32 buffer, no sim changes. Highest wow-per-effort.
close_reason: Implemented + committed (f3e9468): multiply-darken + stacked additive blur off glow32, Light toggle + Darkness slider, l keybinding

title: Save / load / share scenes
description: Serialize the cells grid (RLE-compressed) into a URL hash + downloadable file so scenes are shareable. Enables a scene gallery.

title: Powder Lab feature roadmap
description: Tracked, prioritized features from the CLAUDE.md roadmap. Solo fun project: visual/shareable wins prioritized over invisible engine work.

title: Per-material heat conductivity (replace global DIFFUSE)
description:
Replace the single global DIFFUSE constant in the heat diffusion stencil (src/sim/Simulation.ts diffuse()) with a per-material conductivity rate, so different materials conduct/lose heat at different speeds.

WHY: fire needs FAST diffusion to spread through wood within its short lifespan, but lava needs SLOW diffusion or it bleeds 700->~156 in a single frame and crusts mid-air. One shared rate (currently DIFFUSE=0.2, tuned for fire) can't satisfy both. Because air is a colder sink than water, no single threshold crusts lava-on-water without also crusting lava-in-air. We currently work around this with a coolant-contact gate in updateLava (lava only solidifies when touching water/steam/ice) — functional but a special case. Real conductivity would let lava cool emergently from any sustained cold (incl. slow surface crust) without petrifying thin air streams, and let the coolant gate be removed.
design: diffuse() uses heatNext[i] = h + DIFFUSE*(sum_neighbors - 4h). Generalize to a per-material rate: read conductivity[cells[i]] (new Float32Array(MAT_COUNT) in materials.ts, same style as density/emitTemp). Decide stencil semantics: simplest is rate per CELL (k[i]); more physical is per-EDGE (harmonic mean of the two cells' conductivities) so an insulator genuinely blocks flow. Keep 4*max(rate) < 1 for stability. Stone/wall could insulate (low k), metal conduct (high k). Once landed, drop the coolant gate + nearCoolant/isCoolant in Simulation.ts and re-tune freezePoint[LAVA].
acceptance_criteria: conductivity table exists and is read in diffuse(); fire still spreads through a wood line at ~current pace; a lava stream falls through open air WITHOUT crusting (the Simulation.test.ts 'does NOT crust in open air' case still holds, ideally without the coolant gate); lava still forms a crust against water; chunk culling still settles (no runaway wakes). Stability preserved.

title: Design direction + redesign pass
description: Establish a real design direction for Powder Lab, then apply a redesign of the UI chrome (toolbar, palette, controls, canvas framing — App.tsx + styles.css; not the sim). Approach: in a FRESH instance, run '/impeccable teach' to derive a design direction/system, then do the redesign pass — likely as part of the same effort. Best done after save/share (8aw) lands so the share UI is included in the pass. Acceptance: a documented design direction + the UI chrome redesigned to match.
close_reason: Implementation pass landed: pixel-native chrome built into src/App.tsx + src/styles.css against DESIGN.md, with src/pixel-icons.tsx (hand-pixeled SVG icons) and src/chip-styles.ts (per-material tile textures). Window frame + titlebar (wordmark + Share/Save/Load icons), textured material tiles with Silkscreen labels/kbd badges + marching active marquee, stepped meter sliders (Brush/Speed), lit toggles (Glow/Light/Temp), Darkness dims while Light off. Accent (--accent/-dk/-lt) follows selected material — verified live re-tint Sand->Lava. Google Fonts added to index.html. Mobile layout + prefers-reduced-motion handled. Pixel-native bans hold (no blur/backdrop/radius). Build green, 13 vitest tests pass, visually verified via Playwright at desktop + mobile. Direction half was already done.

title: Mobile / touch support
description:
Make Powder Lab usable on phones. Mostly groundwork-complete: Pointer Events (touch draws today), touch-action:none on canvas, viewport meta set, responsive canvas via getBoundingClientRect mapping + a @media(max-width:720px) breakpoint.

GAPS:
1. BLOCKER: erase is hard-wired to right-click (e.button===2 in useSimulation.ts ~line 83) — no right-click on touch, so erasing is impossible on mobile. Add an eraser toggle button in the UI (works on both desktop + touch); keep right-click as a desktop shortcut.
2. Layout density at ~375px: palette + sliders + toggles need a real on-device pass; touch targets ~44px min. The 720px breakpoint exists but needs verification.
3. Multi-touch: loop tracks a single pointer.current; guard against a stray second finger glitching the active stroke.
4. Perf: weaker mobile GPUs feel the lighting blur more (see powder-lab-85q source-space blur fix) and the fps-coupled sim runs slower on slow devices (see powder-lab-vql).

Acceptance: can draw AND erase with touch on a phone; toolbar usable at 375px; no scroll/zoom hijack while drawing.

title: Lighting perf: blur in source space, not screen space
description: Dynamic lighting drops Firefox to ~20fps (Chrome unaffected). Cause: render() sets ctx.filter blur at SCREEN resolution (800x600) with radii up to scale*12=48px, 3x/frame; Firefox's canvas2D blur filter lacks Chrome's GPU/Skia acceleration. Fix: blur in SOURCE space — glowCanvas is only 200x150, so blur it there at small radii (screen radius / scale, e.g. 48->12px) then drawImage upscaled with NO filter. ~16x fewer pixels + 1/4 radius; near-identical look after nearest-neighbor upscale. Extra wins: skip the blur passes when emissive-cell count is 0 (track a counter in writeImage); optionally drop 3 passes to 2. Target: Firefox back to ~60fps with lighting on.
close_reason: Blur the glow in source space (200x150) at radius/scale instead of at screen resolution, then upscale bilinear with no ctx.filter. Added blurCanvas + glowPass() helper; track emissive cell count in writeImage() and skip all glow/bloom passes (and the glowData upload) when no fire/lava present. ~16x fewer pixels + 1/4 kernel per pass.

title: Add Biome (lint + format)
description: Add Biome (single Rust binary: lint + format) with a biome.json, replacing the need for ESLint+Prettier. Wire npm scripts. Fits Vite+TS+React.
close_reason: Added Biome (@biomejs/biome 2.4.16) as lint+formatter: biome.json (space/2, single quotes, no semicolons, lineWidth 100, excludes package-lock/.beads/dist), npm scripts lint/format/check, and ran a repo-wide format sweep normalizing the prior Prettier-default reformat noise. npm run build green; npm run lint wired (reports pre-existing findings, non-blocking).

title: Fluid velocity & pressure field
description: Give fluids a velocity field so water sloshes (waves slap walls/splash) and gases pressurize/equalize, replacing one-cell-per-frame spread. Sloshing folded in here. Pairs with heat field. Bigger lift.

title: Image import & reveal
description: Drop a PNG, rasterize to colored sand that falls (map luminance/hue to materials), then let it dissolve/burn. Cheap and shareable. (Merges the two roadmap image entries.)

title: Heat field
description: Parallel temperature grid that diffuses each frame. Replace hard-coded touches-fire/lava ignition checks with real thresholds (ignition/melt/freeze). Collapses special cases into one model; ice/steam/lava become emergent. Real refactor.

title: DX & tooling
description: Developer-experience and maintenance chores.

title: Emergent thermal behaviors (build on heat field)
description:
Further emergent behaviors the heat field (powder-lab-h5j) unlocks once a per-cell temperature scalar + per-material thresholds exist.

WHY: the heat field collapses scripted reactions into one physical model; these extend that model for more depth and visual payoff, and several pair well with lightning (powder-lab-z88).

IDEAS (pick what's fun, not commitments):
- Per-material heat conductivity table (metal conducts fast, wall/stone insulates) — generalizes the uniform DIFFUSE constant into a per-material rate.
- Sand -> glass at high heat (new GLASS material, static solid).
- Lava + rapid water-quench -> obsidian (distinct from slow lava cooling -> stone).
- Wood charring stages before ignition (intermediate scorched state via life/heat).
- Plant growth rate scales with warmth (modulate growPlant() probability by heat[i]).
- Heat-shimmer rendering above very hot cells.

APPROACH: all live in src/sim/Simulation.ts update() cases + src/sim/materials.ts threshold/conductivity tables, reusing the heat[] field and emitTemp/ignitionPoint/meltPoint/boilPoint/freezePoint pattern.

ACCEPTANCE: not a single deliverable — each idea is independently shippable; treat this as a parking bucket. Split into concrete child issues when one is picked up.

title: Decouple sim speed from frame rate (fixed timestep)
description: Sim speed is currently coupled to frame rate: the rAF loop runs sim.step(c.speed) once per rendered frame (useSimulation.ts ~line 121), so the sand falls ~2x faster at 120fps (Chrome) than at 60fps (Firefox / 60Hz panel). Decouple with a fixed-timestep accumulator: accumulate elapsed wall-clock dt and run a fixed number of sim steps per real-time unit, while still rendering every frame, so behavior is identical regardless of refresh rate or browser. Watch for: the 'spiral of death' (clamp max steps/frame), and how it composes with the existing speed slider. Discovered while diagnosing the Firefox 60fps cap.

title: Bump deploy workflow Node
description: deploy.yml uses node-version: 20. Bump to 22/24 ahead of Node 20 EOL. Low-urgency hygiene; actions already on current majors (@v4/v5).

title: GPU backend
description: Move the grid to ping-pong shader textures to push from ~30k cells to millions. LARGEST REWRITE: replaces Simulation.step() entirely while keeping the React/material layers. Aspirational / shader-learning project.

title: Clip export (GIF/WebM)
description: One button to record the canvas to GIF/WebM. Doesn't touch the sim; force-multiplier for sharing flashy moments. Do after lighting/explosions exist.

title: Lightning / electricity
description: Material that arcs between conductive cells (metal/water) along a jagged, branching, flickering path. Best after heat field so arcs ignite/boil/melt via thresholds.

title: Material editor
description: Surface density/flammability/reaction tables as live UI so users invent materials. Requires generalizing the hard-coded update() cases into a data-driven reaction table first. Low ROI without users.

title: Perf: render-side dirty-rect
description: writeImage() scans every cell each frame even when chunks sleep; mirror the sim-side chunk culling render-side. DO NOT TOUCH until actual lag is observed (30k cells is fine now).

title: Reactive audio
description: Web Audio sizzle on water+lava, boom on explosions, fire crackle scaled by burn amount. Parked: polish, only fun once dramatic events exist.

title: Velocity-based explosions
description: Give blasts real momentum so they hurl sand/water/debris outward in an arc that rains back down, instead of flashing in place. Depends on the velocity field.
